interpretation: Dynamic local-field / qSTLS extension of the current correlation backend family
evidence_for: STLS already proves that self-consistent correlation physics matters, so a dynamic extension is scientifically coherent
evidence_against: STLS already reduces the driven LFC-like branch MAE to 0.730 eV; the remaining dominant residual is experiment-facing, not branch-matching

interpretation: Material-specific backend or article-native reproduction path for the Al XRTS observable
evidence_for: QHD fixes the driven RPA-like branch, STLS fixes the driven LFC-like branch, yet neither wins experiment-facing comparison; this points beyond generic UEG free-electron backends
evidence_against: Requires a heavier modelling layer and likely more explicit article-side assumptions

interpretation: Use different existing backends for different physics branches and expose that as an interpretation layer
evidence_for: Current evidence already shows branch specialization: QHD for RPA-like, STLS for LFC-like
evidence_against: Explains backend specialization but does not add missing experiment-facing physics

interpretation: Another heavier many-body backend beyond current STLS/QHD
evidence_for: Could in principle include more physics than present backends
evidence_against: Without a material-specific/article-native target, it risks repeating the same pattern at higher cost: better branch match without solving experiment-facing residual

interpretation: Stop because evidence is insufficient to rank the next step
evidence_for: Would be appropriate only if QHD/STLS results were ambiguous
evidence_against: Current evidence is already strong and internally consistent enough to rank the next step
